required_inputs.role: Respondent-item analytic file validated against schema/wave_response.schema.json before exclusions.
required_inputs.validation_rule: All respondent-item rows must use event_id no_event and item_id must be in the frozen v0.2 somatic allowlist.

required_inputs.role: Frozen variable definitions for QC flags, covariates, validation comparators, and restricted operations variables.
required_inputs.validation_rule: Analysis scripts must fail closed if any named QC, covariate, or validation variable is absent or recoded without a dated addendum.

required_inputs.role: Frozen item wording, response anchors, scoring keys, and construct metadata for all four development items.
required_inputs.validation_rule: The analysis allowlist is exactly the four item IDs and v0.2.0 item versions named in the preregistration.

required_inputs.role: Machine-readable destination for planned evidence, pass-fail decisions, retention recommendations, and reviewer signoff.
required_inputs.validation_rule: Every completed statistic listed in this plan must be written or summarized into its target dossier field before any release-status change.

packages.name: psych
packages.purpose: Polychoric correlations, parallel analysis, ordinal EFA, omega, alpha, and item-total diagnostics.

packages.name: lavaan
packages.purpose: Ordered-categorical CFA and measurement-invariance models using WLSMV.

packages.name: semTools
packages.purpose: Ordinal reliability summaries and invariance helper functions.

packages.name: mirt
packages.purpose: Graded-response IRT calibration, local dependence diagnostics, and multi-group IRT sensitivity models.

packages.name: lordif
packages.purpose: Ordinal logistic DIF screening with effect-size diagnostics.

packages.name: survey
packages.purpose: Raking, weight trimming, weighted descriptive summaries, and weighted sensitivity analyses.

packages.name: mice
packages.purpose: Sensitivity checks for missing non-score covariates when complete-case conclusions are fragile.

software_environment.version_lock_rule: Analyses must record sessionInfo output and package versions exactly; any version substitution requires a dossier addendum showing unchanged primary pass-fail decisions.

randomization_and_seeds.seed_policy: Seeds are fixed before outcome inspection; scripts must set the relevant seed immediately before stochastic procedures and write the seed to the dossier methods notes.

sample_splits.overlap_rule: Respondents in the development pilot are ineligible for the confirmation sample and must be identified by panel ID, survey token, and vendor duplicate controls.
Confirmation respondents must have no respondent, panel, device-fingerprint, or survey-token overlap with the development pilot.

eligibility_rules.rule: Respondent is age 18 or older, resides in the United States, can answer the English survey unaided, and provides informed consent.
A confirmation respondent must not have completed or partially completed the development pilot.

exclusion_rules.rule: Exclude respondents failing the instructed-response attention check, scenario-comprehension check, or somatic-attribution check.
Exclude respondents with total completion time below one-third of the same-sample median after removing clear breakoffs.
Exclude respondents giving the same substantive response to all four ANX-Bench items only when they also fail attention or minimum-reading-time checks.
Exclude respondents missing or giving non-substantive responses on more than 25 percent of administered ANX-Bench items.
Exclude duplicate panel IDs, duplicate survey tokens, confirmed duplicate device fingerprints, unusable survey records, and respondents reporting that they did not understand most scenarios.

non_exclusion_flags.rule: Flag but do not automatically exclude item-level response time below one-third of sample median item time, long-string responding, high Mahalanobis distance, or improbable IRT response patterns.
High baseline general anxiety, sleep sensitivity, and health anxiety sensitivity are covariates and DIF or sensitivity variables, not automatic exclusions.

weighting_policy.primary_estimation: Primary EFA, CFA, reliability, IRT, DIF, invariance, and external-validity pass-fail decisions use unweighted analytic data unless the estimator has a validated survey-weight implementation.
weighting_policy.descriptive_estimation: Weighted and unweighted item distributions are both reported; weighted percentages are treated as population-facing descriptive estimates.
weighting_policy.weight_construction: Raking or post-stratification is performed separately by sample using age group, gender, race and ethnicity, education, Census region, and employment status, with AI exposure and AI-news exposure included when stable margins exist.

trimming.exception_rule: Weights outside 0.30 to 3.00 require a written sensitivity addendum showing unchanged primary validation conclusions.

missing_data_handling.item_distributions: Use all eligible respondents with valid responses to the item being summarized; skipped responses remain missing and are not imputed.
missing_data_handling.factor_models: Use pairwise polychoric or full-information ordinal methods when supported by the estimator and report complete-case sensitivity analyses.
missing_data_handling.reliability: Compute omega and alpha on the retained item set using the same analytic response set as the confirmatory factor model, with complete-case sensitivity.
missing_data_handling.irt: Fit graded-response models on respondents with sufficient retained item responses for stable ordinal calibration; report analytic N and missingness counts by item.
missing_data_handling.external_validity: Use complete-case analysis for each external-validity model and report missingness counts for every comparator and covariate.
missing_data_handling.imputation_policy: Do not impute item responses for primary psychometric evidence; missing-indicator or multiple-imputation sensitivity may be used only for non-score covariates.

efa.estimator: Polychoric correlation matrix with minimum residual or weighted least squares extraction; oblique rotation if more than one factor is extracted.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Carry retained structure to confirmation.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block construct scoring and recommend item-pool revision.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Item may remain in the retained structure.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag item for exclusion unless 0.40 to 0.49 has documented construct-coverage justification.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Cross-loading does not block retention.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag item for revision or exclusion from multi-factor scoring.

cfa.estimator: WLSMV ordered-categorical CFA of the retained EFA structure without confirmation-sample model modifications treated as preregistered.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Meets strong CFA fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: CFI 0.90 to 0.949 requires justification; below 0.90 blocks scored approval.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Meets strong CFA fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: TLI 0.90 to 0.949 requires justification; below 0.90 blocks scored approval.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Meets strong CFA fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: RMSEA 0.061 to 0.080 requires justification; above 0.080 blocks scored approval.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Meets strong CFA fit support.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Blocks scored approval unless reviewer documents limited item-level use only.

omega.estimator: Ordinal coefficient omega for retained item sets with at least three items; ordinal alpha and corrected item-total correlations are secondary comparability statistics.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Reliability may support early scored construct review.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block construct scoring and keep item set development_only.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Reliability may support later headline-scoring review if all other gates pass.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Do not authorize mature headline construct scoring.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Item passes internal-consistency screen.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag item unless retained for preregistered construct coverage with explicit justification.

irt.estimator: Graded response model for the retained item set after dimensionality support, with ordered thresholds, item and test information, local dependence, and response-pattern fit diagnostics.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Item may remain in IRT calibration.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Recommend item revision or exclusion.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Item provides useful trait discrimination.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag item for revision unless information is adequate for intentionally broad coverage.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: No severe redundancy detected.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block simultaneous scored retention of locally dependent duplicate items.

dif.estimator: Ordinal logistic regression DIF with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR 0.05 within the four-item family, plus multi-group IRT or MIMIC sensitivity when the retained IRT model is stable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: No statistically supported DIF after FDR control.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Evaluate practical DIF thresholds before retention.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: No practically meaningful ordinal logistic DIF.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block public comparison use for unresolved item DIF.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Expected-score DIF is below practical significance threshold.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block scored comparison claims unless item is excluded or adjusted.

invariance.estimator: Ordered-categorical multi-group CFA sequence for configural, metric, and scalar invariance, or a preregistered IRT linking alternative when CFA is not stable.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Metric invariance screen passes for the comparison group.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Restrict comparison claims or use validated linking alternative.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Metric invariance screen passes for the comparison group.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Restrict comparison claims or use validated linking alternative.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Scalar invariance screen passes for latent mean comparison.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Group mean comparisons are descriptive only unless linking alternative is validated.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Scalar invariance screen passes for latent mean comparison.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Group mean comparisons are descriptive only unless linking alternative is validated.

external_validity.estimator: Spearman and polyserial associations, latent or factor-score associations when supported, and ordinal or generalized models for avoidance and checking intentions.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Convergent AI-news exposure validity is consistent with preregistration.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Flag possible detachment from AI capability exposure.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Candidate score is not redundant with baseline general anxiety.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Block scored approval unless incremental AI-specific variance is demonstrated.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Avoidance criterion association is practically interpretable.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Criterion-validity gate fails for construct scoring.

pass_fail_thresholds.pass_action: Linear sensitivity model supports incremental validity.
pass_fail_thresholds.fail_action: Require standardized coefficient confidence interval excluding zero in ordinal or generalized model.

validation_gates.pass_action: Construct-level scoring review may proceed if all other gates pass.
validation_gates.fail_action: Only item-level development evidence may be reported.
Flag item for revision or exclusion.

validation_gates.pass_action: Item passes public subgroup missingness gate.
validation_gates.fail_action: Block public subgroup comparison for affected item or subgroup.
Flag item for revision or exclusion.

validation_gates.pass_action: Item has acceptable ordered response spread.
validation_gates.fail_action: Flag item unless intentionally extreme-targeted with adequate IRT information.

validation_gates.pass_action: Public comparison use may proceed if invariance also passes.
validation_gates.fail_action: Block scored public comparisons involving affected item or group.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Compare weighted and unweighted item frequencies, percentages, floor and ceiling concentration, and two-adjacent-category concentration.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: A retention conclusion is fragile if weighting changes a pass-fail distribution gate for any item.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Refit primary descriptive, CFA, reliability, IRT, DIF, and external-validity models excluding respondents with non-exclusion QC flags.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: A conclusion is fragile if removing flagged respondents changes any validation gate from pass to fail or fail to pass.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Repeat pilot EFA with alternative extraction methods suitable for polychoric input and compare retained factor count and item loading pattern.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: A proposed factor structure must be substantively stable across plausible ordinal EFA extraction choices.

sensitivity_analyses.description: If an item fails distribution, DIF, or local-dependence thresholds, refit CFA after removing the flagged item while preserving the preregistered rule that at least three retained items are required for construct scoring.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: Improved fit after item removal may support revision but cannot rescue fewer than three retained items for construct scoring.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Repeat DIF screens under alternative reference groups for age, gender, education, prior AI exposure, AI-news exposure, sleep sensitivity, health anxiety sensitivity, and baseline general anxiety.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: DIF is treated as unresolved if practical significance depends on a single arbitrary reference-group choice.

sensitivity_analyses.description: Estimate external-validity models with and without baseline general anxiety, sleep sensitivity, and health anxiety sensitivity covariates.
sensitivity_analyses.primary_comparison: Construct scoring requires the AI-specific candidate score to retain interpretable criterion or incremental validity after core covariate adjustment.

dossier_output.write_policy: Planned fields may remain status planned before fielding; after analysis, each statistic must be written to the named dossier field or the reviewer must record why the statistic was not estimable.

statistic_to_dossier_field.decision_use: Verifies that pilot EFA and preliminary diagnostics used the preregistered development target.
Verifies that confirmatory psychometric gates use the independent preregistered target.
Determines whether completed models are based on the eligible post-exclusion analytic sample.
Documents the CONSORT-style exclusion flow and subgroup exclusion rates.
Confirms that weights follow the 0.30 to 3.00 trimming policy or have a sensitivity addendum.
Supports response-spread, floor, ceiling, and adjacent-category retention gates.
Applies confirmation and public subgroup missingness thresholds.
Documents item-level speed flags and non-exclusion QC sensitivity.
Determines whether the expected one-factor structure or a predeclared interpretable two-factor structure proceeds to CFA.
Applies the primary loading retention threshold for each item.
Applies secondary loading and near-equal loading exclusion rules.
Identifies items that fail to share sufficient common variance with the candidate construct.
Applies the CFI strong-fit and blocking thresholds for construct scoring.
Applies the TLI strong-fit and blocking thresholds for construct scoring.
Applies the RMSEA strong-fit and blocking thresholds for construct scoring.
Applies the SRMR threshold for ordered-categorical model residual fit.
Confirms item retention in the independent confirmation sample.
Applies early scored and mature headline reliability thresholds.
Provides comparability evidence while keeping omega as the primary reliability statistic.
Applies item-total retention thresholds.
Documents expected measurement precision for any later score proposal.
Identifies items with inadequate graded-response discrimination.
Applies ordered-threshold decision rules.
Determines whether each item contributes information across the observed anxiety range.
Summarizes latent-trait precision for the retained item set.
Blocks redundant item pairs from simultaneous scored retention.
Documents improbable patterns and informs QC sensitivity.
Determines whether planned subgroup DIF screens are estimable.
Applies Benjamini-Hochberg FDR screening.
Applies the practical DIF effect-size threshold.
Applies the expected-score practical significance threshold.
Blocks public comparison use when DIF materially changes group rank ordering.
Determines whether metric and scalar invariance tests are interpretable.
Applies the metric invariance CFI threshold.
Applies the metric invariance RMSEA threshold.
Applies the scalar invariance CFI threshold for latent mean comparison.
Applies the scalar invariance RMSEA threshold for latent mean comparison.
Tests preregistered convergent validity with AI-news exposure.
Tests discriminant validity from baseline general anxiety and redundancy threshold.
Tests sleep-specific validity for sleep_disruption_ai_news after covariate adjustment.
Tests bodily-vigilance specificity for body_vigilance_model_release while preserving factor loading requirements.
Applies the preregistered avoidance criterion-validity threshold.
Reports checking intention as a secondary behavioral comparator.
Applies linear sensitivity-model incremental validity threshold.
Applies ordinal or generalized-model incremental validity threshold when R-squared is not primary.
Blocks construct-level scoring if fewer than three items are retained.
Records development_only, approved_item_level_only, approved_scored, or blocked status after all gates.
Prevents official ANX scoring unless every required gate and reviewer signoff passes.
